name: STOA AI Factory — Competitive Watch (2-Phase)

meta.description: Template documentation for the n8n competitive watch workflow. 2 phases: Collect (2x/day) + Digest (weekly Monday). Import into n8n manually.
meta.author: STOA AI Factory

steps.description: Fetch Anthropic engineering blog for new posts
Check latest Claude Code npm version
Fetch top posts from r/ClaudeAI this week
Search Hacker News for Claude Code mentions
Append new signals to JSONL file (dedup by source+title). Equivalent of watch-collect.sh.
steps.pseudocode: For each HTTP response, extract signals → deduplicate against existing signals-YYYY-WNN.jsonl → append new lines

steps.description: Read and deduplicate signals from current week JSONL file
steps.pseudocode: Read .claude/watch/signals-YYYY-WNN.jsonl → deduplicate by source+title → sort by score → group by source

steps.description: Format signals into digest using SKILL.md template
steps.pseudocode: Group signals into BREAKING/NOUVEAUTES/CONCURRENCE/COMMUNAUTE/PATTERNS → format markdown → detect action items

steps.description: If BREAKING items found, create Linear ticket with action items
steps.condition: digest contains BREAKING items with score >= 200

action.description: Create Linear issue with action items from digest

setup_notes: 1. Import this workflow concept into n8n at n8n.gostoa.dev
2. Create 2 workflows: one for Phase 1 (Collect, 2x/day), one for Phase 2 (Digest, Monday)
3. Configure HTTP Request nodes with actual URLs
4. Set up Slack webhook for #stoa-ai-watch channel
5. Optional: configure Linear API key for auto-ticket creation
6. Test with manual trigger before enabling schedules
7. Alternative: use cron/launchd with watch-collect.sh + watch-digest.sh (see CRON-SETUP.md)
